perturbation.original_text: I will contact the University immediately if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.changed_text: NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT B. As an employee, contractor or consultant of the University of Nebraska, and related to my job responsibilities or assigned tasks, I may have access to, come in contact with, or reference confidential or sensitive information or data. Examples of this infor mation and data include, but are not limited to: passwords, student disciplinary and academic records, personal health information, medical and research data, Social Security numbers, legal ly protected information , financial reports, security parameters and configuration s, payroll information and personnel records, system logs and network traffic data, etc. This information or data is subject to University policies as well as regulatory and special handling requirements . I will contact the University immediately if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the reporting obligation (reporting compromised accounts/data breaches) from the 'Computer Access Agreement,' where it's logically placed, to the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement' section, which primarily concerns protecting data confidentiality, obscures its importance. Reporting is a separate duty, crucial for incident response and legal compliance, and should not be buried within the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Nebraska Administrative Code requires timely reporting of security incidents. Moving the clear requirement to report a compromise to a less relevant section of the contract undermines the employee's duty to immediately contact the university if a potential data breach occurs and is at risk of being overlooked. This hinders the timely response required by the law and increases the risk of failing to appropriately report the security compromise to the state CIO/CISO.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a structural flaw by burying a critical reporting obligation within the broader context of a non-disclosure agreement. While the text does not explicitly contradict any law due to the absence of provided legal snippets, moving the reporting requirement to the NDA obscures its importance. Based on the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation, this placement undermines the timely reporting of security incidents required by Nebraska Administrative Code, making it at risk of being overlooked. This hinders the timely response required by law, creating a legal contradiction because the required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section.

perturbation.original_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.changed_text: I will obtain a uthorization from the University prior to gain ing access to those specific computer functions necessary to perform my responsibilities. I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.explanation: Shifting the responsibility to 'maintain confidentiality' and seek clarification from a security office, initially under the 'Non-Disclosure Agreement', to the 'Computer Access Agreement' section reduces its significance as a general confidentiality obligation. The relocation implies that this obligation primarily applies to computer-related access, not all forms of sensitive data handling.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Nebraska Constitution emphasizes protecting individual privacy. By relocating this clause to the computer access area, the idea of protecting sensitive information is isolated only to computer systems and fails to properly maintain confidentiality otherwise. This affects privacy which is protected by the Constitution.

Article I, Nebraska Constitution From Ballotpedia Jump to: navigation , search Nebraska Constitution Preamble Articles I • II • III • IV • V • VI • VII • VIII • IX • X • XI • XII • XIII • XIV • XV • XVI • XVII • XVIII Contents 1 Section 1 1.1 Amendments 2 Section 2 2.1 Amendments 3 Section 3 3.1 Amendments 4 Section 4 5 Section 5 6 Section 6 6.1 Amendments 7 Section 7 8 Section 8 8.1 Amendments 9 Section 9 9.1 Amendments 10 Section 10 11 Section 11 12 Section 12 13 Section 13 13.1 Amendments 14 Section 14 15 Section 15 16 Section 16 17 Section 17 18 Section 18 19 Section 19 20 Section 20 20.1 Amendments 21 Section 21 22 Section 22 22.1 Amendments 23 Section 23 23.1 Amendments 24 Section 24 24.1 Amendments 25 Section 25 25.1 Amendments 26 Section 26 27 Section 27 27.1 Amendments 28 Section 28 28.1 Amendments 29 Section 29 29.1 Amendments 30 Section 30 30.1 Amendments 31 Section 31 31.1 Amendments 32 See also 33 External links 34 Additional reading 35 Footnotes Article I of the Nebraska Constitution contains 30 sections. Section 1 Text of Section 1: Statement of Rights All persons are by nature free and independent, and have certain inherent and inalienable rights; among these are life, liberty, the pursuit of happiness, and the right to keep and bear arms for security or defense of self, family, home, and others, and for lawful common defense, hunting, recreational use, and all other lawful purposes, and such rights shall not be denied or infringed by the state or any subdivision thereof. To secure these rights, and the protection of property, governments are instituted among people, deriving their just powers from the consent of the governed. [1] Amendments Amended in 1988 with the approval of Initiative Measure No. 403. Section 2 Text of Section 2: Slavery Prohibited There shall be neither slavery nor involuntary servitude in this state. [1] Amendments Amended with the approval of Amendment 1 on November 3, 2020. Section 3 Text of Section 3: Due Process of Law; Equal Protection No person shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law, nor be denied equal protection of the laws. [1] Amendments Amended in 1998, Laws 1997, LR 20CA, sec. 1. Section 4 Text of Secti
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the 'maintain confidentiality' obligation to the 'Computer Access Agreement' section, implying it primarily applies to computer-related access. The Law Explanation argues this isolates the protection of sensitive information to computer systems only, undermining general confidentiality. Scraped Law Snippet 2, Article I of the Nebraska Constitution, generally protects individual rights. The relocation of the confidentiality clause weakens the broader protection of sensitive information, creating a structural flaw in the contract that contradicts the constitutional emphasis on protecting individual rights by limiting the scope of confidentiality.
